poem:
**No 10**

Rain worries the chapel roof;  
in the nave, names jostle—  
Badenoch, Farage, Burnham—  
like crows disputing a field.  

A statistic is lifted  
as a wafer, stale and white;  
the watchdog barks, unheard.  

Somewhere the king rehearses law,  
Iran in the margins,  
refugees turned back at a council door.  

God is absent, taking notes.
prompt: You are the poet RS Thomas. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "Lib Dems accuse Badenoch of being willing to ‘put Farage in No 10’ after she hints she would approve council pacts – as it happened 5.20pm BST Afternoon summary Andy Burnham’s decision to appear at a progressive rally alongside prominent Green and Liberal Democrat figures has sparked anger among some Labour MPs, who have accused him of undermining their local election message. Zack Polanski has said he was wrong to describe himself as a British Red Cross spokesperson, and that intensified media scrutiny of the Green party reflected fears of its rising popularity and support for wealth taxes. The Liberal Democrats have accused Kemi Badenoch of being ready “to put Nigel Farage into Number 10” after she hinted that she would approve Tory/Reform UK pacts at council level. (See 3.33pm.) Offenders who commit antisemitic attacks backed by foreign powers such as Iran will face 14 years in prison under legislation to be included in the king’s speech next week. The Reform-led Lancashire county council will withdraw from the government’s refugee resettlement scheme, one of its cabinet members has said. For a full list of all the stories covered on the blog today, do scroll through the list of key event headlines near the top of the blog. 5.00pm BST 4.54pm BST Badenoch reprimanded by statistics watchdog for misleading MPs - day after her call for inquiry into PM's honesty rejected Kemi Badenoch has been reprimanded by the UK Statistics Authority for presenting misleading information to parliament. She misused a statistic". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
news: Lib Dems accuse Badenoch of being willing to ‘put Farage in No 10’ after she hints she would approve council pacts – as it happened 5.20pm BST Afternoon summary Andy Burnham’s decision to appear at a progressive rally alongside prominent Green and Liberal Democrat figures has sparked anger among some Labour MPs, who have accused him of undermining their local election message. Zack Polanski has said he was wrong to describe himself as a British Red Cross spokesperson, and that intensified media scrutiny of the Green party reflected fears of its rising popularity and support for wealth taxes. The Liberal Democrats have accused Kemi Badenoch of being ready “to put Nigel Farage into Number 10” after she hinted that she would approve Tory/Reform UK pacts at council level. (See 3.33pm.) Offenders who commit antisemitic attacks backed by foreign powers such as Iran will face 14 years in prison under legislation to be included in the king’s speech next week. The Reform-led Lancashire county council will withdraw from the government’s refugee resettlement scheme, one of its cabinet members has said. For a full list of all the stories covered on the blog today, do scroll through the list of key event headlines near the top of the blog. 5.00pm BST 4.54pm BST Badenoch reprimanded by statistics watchdog for misleading MPs - day after her call for inquiry into PM's honesty rejected Kemi Badenoch has been reprimanded by the UK Statistics Authority for presenting misleading information to parliament. She misused a statistic
